name: Qwen3.5 0.8B
summary: Very small Qwen3.5 option for older phones and fast downloads. Good fallback when storage and memory are tight.

name: Qwen3.5 2B Opus Distilled
summary: Distilled Qwen3.5 2B variant aimed at sharper Claude-style reasoning, coding help, and cleaner follow-through than the base 2B while staying phone-friendly.

name: Qwen3.5 2B
summary: Strong small-model upgrade over fallback tiers. This is a good default target for mainstream phones.

name: Qwen3.5 2B Tuned
summary: Qwen3.5 2B variant tuned for more direct, less filtered replies than the base 2B while staying phone-friendly.

name: Hermes 3 Llama 3.2 3B
summary: Compact Hermes model with strong tool-calling and instruction following. Great for mobile devices. Supports structured function calling via ChatML.

name: Granite 4.1 3B
summary: IBM Granite 4.1 3B with 131K context, strong tool-calling, and multilingual support across 12 languages. Apache 2.0. No vision.

name: Qwen3.5 4B Tuned
summary: Qwen3.5 4B variant tuned for more direct, less filtered replies than the base 4B while staying light enough for newer phones, tablets, and desktops.

name: Qwopus3.5 4B v3
summary: Reasoning-enhanced Qwen3.5 4B with act-then-refine agent workflows. Strong coding (HumanEval 75.6%) and tool-calling for its size.

name: Qwen3.5 4B
summary: Higher-quality Qwen3.5 option for newer phones. Likely one of the best mainstream chat models in the catalog.

name: Opus4.7 GODs Ghost Codex 4B
summary: Code-focused Qwen3.5 4B fine-tune trained on a mix of Claude Opus reasoning datasets and a 'GhostCoder' code corpus. Aimed at Python/JS/C++ generation, debugging, and refactoring locally.

name: Gemma 4 E2B IT
summary: Ultra-compact Gemma 4 with vision and 128K context. Google's smallest multimodal model — great for older phones and fast downloads.

name: DeepHermes 3 Llama 3 8B
summary: Hermes 8B with unified reasoning mode — toggle between fast responses and chain-of-thought thinking within a single model. Tool-calling included.

name: Hermes 3 Llama 3.1 8B
summary: Flagship Hermes 8B with excellent tool-calling, structured output, and multi-turn conversation. Strong all-rounder for phones and desktops.

name: Gemma 4 E4B IT
summary: Mid-range Gemma 4 with vision, 128K context, and strong multilingual support. Sweet spot for mainstream phones and tablets.

name: Gemma 4 E4B Tuned
summary: Gemma 4 E4B variant tuned for more direct, less filtered replies. Natively multimodal (text, image, video, audio) with 128K context.

name: Qwen3.5 9B Tuned
summary: Qwen3.5 9B variant tuned to feel friendlier, more direct, and less inquisitive in everyday chats. Heavy for phones and best suited to newer devices.

name: Qwen3.5 9B Opus Distilled
summary: Distilled Qwen3.5 9B variant aimed at stronger Claude-style reasoning, longer-form answers, and sharper coding help than the base 9B. Heavy for phones and a better fit for newer devices and desktops.

name: Qwopus3.5 9B v3
summary: Reasoning-enhanced Qwen3.5 9B with shorter, more stable reasoning paths. Outperforms base 9B on HumanEval (+4.87 pp) while using 25% fewer reasoning tokens.

name: Qwen3.5 9B Opus Distilled v2
summary: Improved v2 distillation of Qwen3.5 9B with 20% fewer reasoning tokens and higher accuracy. Good for offline analytical tasks, coding, and math.

name: Qwen3.5 9B DeepSeek V4 Flash
summary: Qwen3.5 9B distilled from DeepSeek V4 Flash with emphasis on tool-augmented workflows and reliable agentic action generation. Strong fit for offline agents and structured tool calls.

name: Harmonic Hermes 9B
summary: Qwen3.5 9B fine-tune combining deep reasoning with Hermes-style tool calling and agent workflows. Vision-capable with 8K context.

name: Carnice 9B
summary: Qwen3.5 9B fine-tuned for Hermes-style agent workflows — terminal, file, and browser tool trajectories with multi-turn tool calling and reasoning repair.

name: Qwen3.5 9B
summary: Stretch goal model for flagship phones, tablets, and desktop-class devices. Expect heavier memory pressure and slower responses.

name: Talkie 1930 13B
summary: Vintage 1930s-style language model trained on early-20th-century text. Produces period-flavored prose, not a general assistant. macOS only — first-mover GGUF port by PocketAI.

name: Qwen3.5 27B Opus Distilled
summary: Desktop-focused Qwen3.5 27B variant distilled toward Claude-style reasoning and longer-form responses. Best suited to Macs and PCs with substantial unified memory or RAM.

name: Qwen3.5 27B Tuned
summary: Desktop-focused Qwen3.5 27B variant tuned for more direct and less filtered responses than the base 27B. Best suited to Macs and PCs with substantial memory headroom.

name: Qwen3.5 27B Opus Distilled v2
summary: Improved v2 distillation of Qwen3.5 27B with ~24% shorter chain-of-thought and 31.6% more correct solutions per token. 96.91% HumanEval pass@1. Desktop only.

name: Qwopus3.5 27B v3
summary: Reasoning-enhanced Qwen3.5 27B with tool-calling RL and act-then-refine agentic workflows. 95.73% HumanEval Plus. Best for desktop setups with high memory.

name: Gemopus 4 26B-A4B
summary: Gemma 4 26B-A4B fine-tuned for higher answer quality with chain-of-thought reasoning. MoE with only 4B active params. Vision-capable. Desktop only.

name: Gemma 4 26B-A4B IT
summary: Mixture-of-Experts Gemma 4 with 25.2B total params but only 3.8B active per token. Flagship-quality vision and 256K context in a memory-efficient MoE package.

name: Gemma 4 26B-A4B Tuned
summary: Gemma 4 26B-A4B variant tuned for more direct replies. MoE architecture with only 4B active params. Vision support via dedicated mmproj.

name: SuperGemma4 26B Uncensored
summary: Uncensored Gemma 4 26B-A4B MoE variant tuned for direct, unfiltered chat. Only 4B active params. Desktop only due to size.

name: Qwen3.5 27B
summary: Desktop-class Qwen3.5 model tuned for stronger reasoning and coding quality. Intended for higher-memory laptops and workstations.

name: GLM-4.7 Flash Opus Distilled
summary: Large desktop-focused GLM variant distilled toward stronger Claude-style reasoning and longer-form answers. Best suited to Macs and PCs with high memory budgets.

name: Granite 4.1 30B
summary: IBM's flagship Granite 4.1 dense 30B model with 128K context. Strong reasoning, instruction following, and enterprise-friendly Apache 2.0 licensing. Desktop only.

name: Gemma 4 31B IT
summary: Google's flagship Gemma 4 dense model. 30.7B params, 256K context, vision, and top-tier benchmarks (MMLU Pro 85.2%, AIME 89.2%). Desktop only.

name: Gemma 4 31B Tuned
summary: Gemma 4 31B variant tuned for more direct responses. Full dense 31B architecture for maximum quality. Desktop only.

name: Hermes 4.3 36B
summary: Latest flagship Hermes model based on ByteDance Seed 36B. 512K context window, best-in-class tool-calling, and strong reasoning. Desktop only.

name: Qwen3.5 35B-A3B
summary: Large Mixture-of-Experts Qwen3.5 option for high-end desktop setups where quality matters more than memory footprint.

name: Qwen3.6 35B-A3B
summary: Qwen 3.6 MoE with Gated DeltaNet architecture: 35B total but only 3B active per token. Vision-capable with 262K context. Successor to Qwen3.5-35B-A3B.

name: Qwen3.6 27B
summary: Dense Qwen 3.6 27B with Gated DeltaNet + Gated Attention hybrid. Reasoning-first, vision-capable, 262K native context. Desktop only — best on 24GB+ VRAM or high-memory Apple Silicon.

name: Qwen3.6 27B Tuned
summary: Qwen3.6 27B dense variant tuned for more direct and less filtered responses than the base 27B. Vision-capable with 262K native context. Desktop only — best on 24GB+ VRAM or high-memory Apple Silicon.

name: Carnice V2 27B
summary: Qwen3.6 27B SFT-merged fine-tune optimized for Hermes-style agent traces and multi-turn tool workflows. Desktop only — best on 24GB+ VRAM or high-memory Apple Silicon.

name: Qwopus3.6 27B v1 Preview
summary: Reasoning-focused fine-tune of Qwen3.6 27B on ~12K curated examples. Dense vision-capable model with 262K native context. Desktop only.

name: Ornstein3.6 35B-A3B
summary: Community-tuned Qwen 3.6 MoE fine-tune with 35B total / 3B active params. Optimized for creative writing and open-ended conversation. Desktop only.

name: Qwen3 Coder Next 80B-A3B
summary: MoE coding-specialist model with only 3B active parameters but 80B total. Excels at long-horizon reasoning, tool use, and agentic coding tasks. Desktop-only due to large memory footprint.

name: Qwen3.5 122B-A10B
summary: Ultra-large desktop MoE model for workstation-class hardware with very high memory capacity.

name: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Super 120B-A12B
summary: High-end Nemotron MoE option for workstation-class Macs and PCs. Expect long downloads, long load times, and a very large memory footprint.

name: MiniMax M2.7
summary: 229B dense model from MiniMax. Massive memory footprint but competitive with frontier models. Requires workstation-class hardware with 140+ GB available.

name: Qwen3.5 REAP 262B-A17B
summary: Pruned Qwen3.5-397B variant using REAP (Router-weighted Expert Activation Pruning) to cut 35% of MoE experts. Near-397B quality in a smaller footprint, fits on a single 96 GB GPU with CPU offload.

name: Qwen3.5 397B-A17B
summary: Maximum-scale Qwen3.5 desktop option for enterprise-grade hardware and very large memory headroom.

name: Qwen3.5 9B GLM5.1 Distilled
summary: Distilled Qwen3.5 9B variant trained on GLM-5.1 reasoning traces. Aims for stronger structured reasoning and step-by-step problem solving than the base 9B. Heavy for phones and better suited to newer devices.

name: Qwen3.6 35B-A3B Tuned
summary: Qwen3.6 35B-A3B variant tuned to feel friendlier, more direct, and less inquisitive in everyday chats. Same 262K context as the base. Desktop-class memory footprint.

name: Qwen3.6 35B-A3B Opus Distilled
summary: Qwen3.6 35B-A3B fine-tuned on Claude Opus 4.6-style chain-of-thought reasoning traces. Sharper structured reasoning and longer-form answers than the base. Desktop-class memory footprint.

name: Kimi K2.6 1T-A32B
summary: Flagship Kimi K2.6 Mixture-of-Experts with 1T total and 32B active parameters. 384 experts, 256K native context, MoonViT vision encoder. Datacenter-class memory footprint — only for multi-GPU workstations or servers.

name: GLM-5.1
summary: Flagship GLM-5.1 Mixture-of-Experts (754B total parameters, glm_moe_dsa architecture) targeted at agentic engineering and code generation. 202K native context. Datacenter-class memory footprint — only for multi-GPU workstations or servers.

name: Qwen3.5 9B MTP
summary: Qwen3.5 9B with Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) head bundled in the GGUF. Same dense base model and 32K context as Qwen3.5 9B; the extra MTP layer is used by llama.cpp builds with speculative decoding for faster generation, and is harmlessly ignored otherwise.

name: Qwopus3.5 9B Coder
summary: Qwen3.5 9B distilled into a coding-focused variant in the Qwopus family. Vision-capable with 32K context. Strong fit for offline coding assistance on flagship phones and desktops.

name: Qwopus3.5 9B Coder MTP
summary: Qwopus3.5 9B Coder with Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) head bundled in the GGUF for faster generation via speculative decoding in supported llama.cpp builds. Identical base behavior to Qwopus3.5 9B Coder otherwise.

name: Qwen3.6 35B-A3B MTP
summary: Qwen3.6 35B-A3B MoE (35B total / 3B active) with Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) head bundled in the GGUF. Same vision-capable base model and 262K context as Qwen3.6 35B-A3B; the extra MTP layer enables speculative decoding in supported llama.cpp builds. Desktop only.

name: Qwen3.6 27B MTP
summary: Qwen3.6 27B dense model with Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) head bundled in the GGUF. Same vision-capable base, Gated DeltaNet + Gated Attention hybrid, and 262K context as Qwen3.6 27B; the extra MTP layer enables speculative decoding in supported llama.cpp builds. Desktop only — best on 24GB+ VRAM or high-memory Apple Silicon.

name: Qwopus3.6 27B v2
summary: Second-generation reasoning fine-tune of Qwen3.6 27B with refined distillation data and Claude-style chain-of-thought. Dense vision-capable model with 262K native context. Desktop only.

name: DeepSeek V4 Flash
summary: DeepSeek V4 Flash (284B MoE) quantized by antirez using asymmetric quantization — routed experts at IQ2_XXS/Q4_K, attention and routing components preserved at Q8_0/F16. Tuned for the DS4 inference engine; may run in recent llama.cpp builds but not guaranteed. Workstation-class only: 128 GB RAM minimum for the IQ2_XXS variant.

name: NousCoder 14B
summary: 14B dense code-generation model from Nous Research, fine-tuned for software engineering, agent workflows, and tool use. Mid-size sibling between phone-friendly 9B coders and the desktop-only 30B+ MoE coders.

name: Hermes 4 14B
summary: Hermes 4 instruction model at 14B scale — balanced reasoning, tool calling, and direct instruction-following. Mid-size sibling between Hermes 3 8B and Hermes 4.3 36B, well-suited to 12GB+ VRAM GPUs and flagship Apple Silicon.

name: Qwen3 Coder 30B-A3B Instruct
summary: Qwen3 Coder MoE: 30B total / 3B active parameters, optimized for code generation, repo-scale agent workflows, and tool use across many programming languages. 256K native context, MoE shape fits the 3090's 24GB VRAM comfortably. Desktop only.

name: Qwopus3.5 4B Coder MTP
summary: Compact Qwopus coder model with a Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) head for faster generation in supported llama.cpp builds. Good offline coding assistant for phones, tablets, and desktops.

name: LFM2.5 8B-A1B
summary: Liquid AI MoE chat model with about 1B active parameters per token and 128K context. Fast local assistant for coding, tool use, and general chat.

name: Gemma 4 12B IT
summary: Dense Gemma 4 instruct model with vision, multilingual support, and 128K context. A strong mid-large multimodal option for high-memory phones and desktops.

name: Qwopus3.6 27B v2 MTP
summary: Qwopus3.6 27B v2 reasoning fine-tune with a Multi-Token Prediction (MTP) head for speculative decoding in supported llama.cpp builds. Desktop-focused and vision-capable.

name: Qwen3.6 40B Claude Opus Deckard Heretic
summary: Qwen3.6 40B community tune distilled from Claude Opus-style reasoning data, with thinking and coding emphasis. Large desktop-only model with vision projector available.

name: Step 3.7 Flash
summary: Very large Step 3.7 Flash vision-language model with 256K context and selectable reasoning behavior. IQ3_XXS multipart GGUF; workstation-class hardware only.
